##NAMES=Hydroredmondite
##RRUFFID=R250068
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[Pb_8_O_2_Zn(OH)_6_](S_2_O_3_)_4_·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Redmond mine, Haywood County, North Carolina, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless, transparent prisms, blades, and tablets. It occurs together with redmondite (R250069) and  sulfatoredmondite (R250070) in a highly unusual secondary assemblage that comprises a variety of rare Pb-Zn-Cu sulfates, thiosulfates, and carbonates. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2023)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250068
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[Pb_8.07_O_2_Zn_0.99_(OH)_6_](S_2_O_3_)_3.91_(SO_4_)_0.09_·2H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.5991 b: 9.2819 c: 12.9774 alpha: 90 beta: 90.443 gamma: 90 volume: 1517.57 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
